_comment_top: Reference: the schema of ~/.win_computer_use/config.json. The MCP server seeds this file on first run from config.default.json at the repo root. The agent can read/modify these fields at runtime via the tools noted in inline comments.
_comment_bypass: If true, the AI may launch any executable without the allowlist gate. Toggle at runtime via permission_set_bypass(enabled). Restore to false at session end.
_comment_allowed_apps: Exe basenames (case-insensitive) the AI may launch when bypass=false. Add at runtime via permission_add_allowed_app(app_name).
_comment_blocked_apps: Exe basenames the AI is never allowed to launch, even with bypass=true. Hard-coded refusal.
_comment_max_screenshot_dim: Largest dimension (px) returned by screenshot tools. Larger captures are scaled down. Lower this if you hit token caps frequently.
_comment_mouse_move_duration_s: Default virtual-cursor animation time in seconds. Settable at runtime via set_speed(duration_s).
_comment_fail_safe: Maps to pyautogui.FAILSAFE. If the real cursor reaches a screen corner, the next call aborts. DO NOT DISABLE.
_comment_agent_name: Label shown next to the overlay cursor. Settable at runtime via set_agent_name(name).
_comment_cursor_color: Overlay ring color (hex). Settable at runtime via set_cursor_color(hex_color).
_comment_overlay_enabled: Whether the labeled overlay cursor is shown. Settable at runtime via overlay_show(on). DO NOT TURN OFF without telling the user - it is their only live signal.
_comment_showcase_mode: Slow watchable motion. Settable at runtime via set_showcase_mode(on). Recommended on when a human is watching.
_comment_emergency_hotkey: Global panic hotkey. When pressed, freezes all input-bearing tools until emergency_resume() is called.
